title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Friday, March 20, 2026, Market Close & Weekend Update

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Decline for Fourth Consecutive Week Amid Surging Oil Prices and Inflationary Concerns
items.summary: U.S. equity markets closed sharply lower on Friday, marking a fourth straight week of declines, as escalating tensions in the Middle East drove oil prices higher and dampened hopes for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. The S&P 500, Nasdaq Composite, and Dow Jones Industrial Average all saw significant drops, with market sentiment characterized by 'risk-off' trading. The Volatility Index (VIX) surged, reflecting increased investor anxiety.

items.headline: FedEx Surges on Strong Q1 Earnings Beat
items.summary: FedEx ($FDX) jumped 9% after reporting better-than-expected Q1 fiscal year 2026 earnings, with EPS of $5.25 against a $4.12 consensus and revenue of $24 billion surpassing forecasts of $23.44 billion. The company also raised its full-year earnings guidance.

items.headline: Planet Labs Rockets on Q4 Performance
items.summary: Earth observation specialist Planet Labs PBC ($PL) saw a significant gain, rising 26% after reporting breakeven adjusted EPS in Q4 and 41% revenue growth, both exceeding Wall Street's expectations.

items.headline: Torrid Holdings Rises on Strategic Moves and Strong Q4 Revenue
items.summary: Torrid Holdings Inc. ($CURV) saw its stock trending up by 22.4% following positive investor sentiment, outperforming Q4 revenue predictions, and strategic moves like closing underperforming stores and launching new sub-brands.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Plummets Amid CEO Charges
items.summary: Super Micro Computer, Inc. ($SMCI) dropped 33.32% as it faced ongoing pressure following chip smuggling charges against its CEO.

items.headline: U Power Limited Crashes on Dilutive Public Offering
items.summary: U Power Limited ($UCAR) fell 65.08% after announcing and pricing a $6 million public offering of units at a significant discount, leading to panic selling due to dilution concerns.

items.headline: Reviva Pharma Holdings Dips on Discounted Offering
items.summary: Reviva Pharmaceuticals Holdings, Inc. ($RVPH) declined 55.43% after pricing a $10 million public offering of shares plus warrants at a discount, following recent weakness and a reverse split.

items.headline: Energy and Financial Services Lead in a Down Market
items.summary: In a generally declining market, the energy sector showed strength, climbing 2.99%, while financial services also saw a modest gain of 0.35%. This comes as oil prices surged and the Federal Reserve maintained interest rates.

items.headline: Utilities and Basic Materials Lag Amid Broader Weakness
items.summary: The utilities sector was the worst performer, down 5.0%, followed by basic materials, which fell 4.82%. Consumer discretionary also experienced weakness as investors shifted towards more defensive stocks.

items.headline: Industrials and Utilities Poised for 2026 Growth
items.summary: Looking ahead, the Industrials sector is expected to have another strong year in 2026 driven by infrastructure demand. Utilities are also anticipated to benefit from increased demand from data centers and the need to update aging electric infrastructure.

items.headline: Fed Signals Potential Rate Hikes as Inflation and Oil Prices Soar
items.summary: Despite calls for interest rate cuts, the Federal Reserve may be forced to raise rates due to persistent inflation, surging oil prices, and shifting market expectations. The Fed maintained rates between 3.50% and 3.75% earlier this week and also raised its GDP growth and core PCE inflation projections for 2026-2028.

items.headline: Mortgage Rates Hit 6-Month High Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: On Saturday, March 21, 2026, mortgage and refinance interest rates reached their highest point in six months, with the popular 30-year fixed mortgage rate climbing to 6.31%. This rise reflects ongoing inflationary pressures and global market instability.

items.headline: Iran War Intensifies Market Pressure and Oil Volatility
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly involving Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz, continues to be a primary driver of market sentiment, leading to heightened oil price volatility. Brent crude prices were hovering around $112 a barrel. The conflict poses a significant risk for the global economy, with analysts suggesting oil prices sustained at $140 a barrel could trigger a U.S. recession.

items.headline: Accenture and Micron Technology Post Strong Earnings
items.summary: Accenture ($ACN) reported Q2 results that exceeded analyst expectations, boosted by demand for AI-related services, and raised its fiscal year 2026 outlook. Micron Technology ($MU) also saw a sharp increase in earnings, driven by strong demand for memory chips in AI and data centers.
